3 hours per week in the gym. that's it. 3 sessions x 1 hour. monday wednesday friday. compound lifts only. i gained 12lbs of muscle in 8 months on this. you don't need 6 days.

#fitness #gym #training

the protein myth needs to die. you don't need 1g per lb of bodyweight. 0.7g per lb is the actual research consensus. for a 180lb guy that's 126g not 180g. stop force-feeding yourself.

#protein #nutrition #fitness

creatine monohydrate. 5g per day. every day. forever. $0.03 per serving. the single most researched supplement in history with the most consistent results. stop buying everything else first.

#creatine #supplements #fitness

what's your one non-negotiable exercise? the one you'd keep if you could only do ONE lift forever. mine is the squat.

tracked every workout for 365 days. the exercises that built the most muscle: barbell squat, barbell bench, barbell row, overhead press, deadlift. 5 exercises. everything else is accessory.

sleep is the most anabolic thing you're not doing enough of. 7-9 hours. not negotiable. i gained more muscle in 3 months of prioritizing sleep than 6 months of perfect training with 5 hours of sleep.

#sleep #recovery #gains

progressive overload is the only thing that matters. add 2.5lbs to the bar every week. that's 130lbs in a year. most people lift the same weight for 3 years then wonder why they look the same.

#progressiveoverload #strength

unpopular fitness opinion: abs are made in the kitchen is only half right. abs are revealed in the kitchen and built in the gym. you need both.

thoughts?

when do you train?

walking 10,000 steps per day burned more fat than any cardio routine i've tried. no cortisol spike. no muscle loss. no gym time wasted. just walk more. seriously.

#walking #fatloss #cardio

cold shower every morning for 60 days. here's what actually happened: nothing magical. but my willpower for the gym went up because I'd already done something hard before 7am. it's a discipline primer.

#coldshower #discipline #fitness
you can only eat 3 foods for the rest of your life to stay in shape. what are they? mine: chicken, rice, broccoli. boring but effective.

electrolytes in the morning before coffee changed my workouts. pinch of salt, squeeze of lemon, 16oz water. zero cost. more energy than any pre-workout. your morning dehydration is killing your performance.

#electrolytes #hydration #performance
leg day yesterday. stairs are not my friend today. my 4 year old asked why i'm walking funny. told her daddy made poor life choices. she said 'again?'

the 80/20 of fat loss: eat in a 500 calorie deficit. that's it. no keto. no intermittent fasting required. no carb cutting. just eat 500 less than you burn. lose 1lb per week like clockwork.

#fatloss #calories #nutrition

front squats fixed my back pain from regular squats. forced me into better posture. lighter weight, same muscle activation, zero pain. sometimes the regression is the progression.

#squat #mobility #gym
be honest: how many days did you actually train last week? no cap. I'll go first: 3 out of planned 4.

#gymanxiety #beginner #fitness
morning training without eating first or evening training with a full day of food? which gives you better lifts?

the bulk/cut cycle is overrated for beginners. just eat at maintenance with high protein and lift heavy. you'll recomp. gained visible muscle while staying the same weight for 6 months.

home gym ROI: spent $800 on a rack, barbell, bench, and 300lbs of plates. gym membership was $50/month. paid for itself in 16 months. now i train in my garage at 5am in my underwear. freedom.

new WalkToUnlock feature: streak tracking. longest current user streak: 43 consecutive days hitting step goals. can you beat it?

download and start

grip strength is the most underrated indicator of overall health. dead hangs from a pull-up bar. start with 20 seconds. work to 60. my back pain disappeared and my deadlift went up 40lbs.

#gripstrength #deadhang #health

rest 3-5 minutes between heavy compound sets. not 60 seconds. you're not doing cardio. you're building strength. longer rest = heavier lifts = more muscle. the science is clear.

#restperiods #strength #hypertrophy
the protein shake or the whole food meal after training? science says it doesn't matter. what do you prefer?

the overhead press is the most honest lift. you can't cheat it. you can't use momentum. you either press it or you don't. add 5lbs per month and watch your shoulders transform.

#overheadpress #shoulders #strength

magnesium glycinate before bed. 400mg. sleep quality went from 6/10 to 8/10. better sleep = better recovery = more muscle. $0.15 per night. most underprescribed supplement.

#magnesium #sleep #recovery

free 30-day bodyweight program. no gym needed. pushups, pullups, squats, planks. progressive overload built in. PDF download.

rest day guilt is real. your muscles literally grow while you rest not while you train. take the day off. you're not losing gains.

who needed to hear this?

what's your gym pet peeve? curling in the squat rack? unsolicited advice? grunting? phone filming? pick one.

vent below

farmer's walks. grab heavy dumbbells. walk 40 yards. rest 60 seconds. repeat 4 times. builds grip, traps, core, and mental toughness in 10 minutes. most underused exercise in every gym.

#farmerswalks #functional #strength

calorie tracking for 30 days then stop. the point isn't to track forever. the point is to learn what 2,500 calories actually looks like. after 30 days you can eyeball it. education not obsession.

#calories #tracking #nutrition

the mind-muscle connection is real. slow your bench press down. 3 seconds down, pause, 2 seconds up. lighter weight. feel the chest working. my chest grew more in 3 months than the previous year.

#mindmuscle #bench #hypertrophy

bulk season or cut season right now? and how's it going? actually how's it going.

hit 225 on bench today. been chasing this number for 11 months. nobody in the gym cared. texted my wife. she said 'nice honey.' this is the loneliest sport.

bodyweight exercises until you can: 20 pushups, 5 pullups, 50 bodyweight squats, 60 second plank. if you can't do these don't touch a barbell yet. build the foundation.

#bodyweight #beginner #foundation
the exercise you hate the most is probably the one you need the most. for me it's lunges. what's yours?

sauna 3x per week after training. 15 minutes at 180F. recovery improved. sleep improved. skin improved. $10/month at my gym. the most underused amenity in every commercial gym.

#sauna #recovery #health

the pump is not growth. soreness is not progress. the only measure: are you lifting heavier this month than last month? yes = growing. no = change something.

#strength #progress #reality

honest admission: i started the fitness account because i was 195lbs, 30% body fat, and couldn't do 5 pushups. that was 8 months ago. now i'm 185lbs, 18% body fat. still not impressive but the direction matters.

fish oil. vitamin D. creatine. magnesium. that's the supplement stack. total cost: $1.20/day. everything else is marketing. don't buy test boosters. don't buy fat burners. save your money.
